body.body:
Emerged from investigation of T-dead-habit-engram-cursor #429 on 2026-04-14. The premise of #429 was wrong — PROC_INVOKE_SPRINT and PROC_INVOKE_COMMIT are not dead. They're aspirational habits waiting on an interpreter that doesn't exist yet.

## Current state

What EXISTS:
- **skill_importer.py** has created 20+ SKILL_*_ENTRY engram memories in the live DB: SKILL_SPRINT_ENTRY, SKILL_COMMIT_ENTRY, SKILL_AUDIT_ENTRY, SKILL_TICKET_ENTRY, SKILL_DAY_CLOSE_ENTRY, SKILL_CONTEXT_LOAD_ENTRY, SKILL_SAVESTATE_ENTRY, etc. Each carries a program payload with run_cell containing MCPCALL + FORKIF + ENDIF primitives parsed from the .claude skill markdown.
- **PROC_INVOKE_SPRINT** (trigger: /sprint|sprint on|start sprint|...) and **PROC_INVOKE_COMMIT** (trigger: /commit|commit and push|...) are habits that pattern-match on natural-language invocations of those skills. They already have the right trigger phrases and the right intent.
- **D260 payload-as-program** is the schema that stores engram programs on memory rows.
- **pe_chain.py run_pe_chain** is an existing chain interpreter but it's scoped to PROC_CODE_A_TICKET — not a general-purpose engram walker.

What is MISSING:
- **pe_chain:run_engram_cursor** — the function both habits reference. Does not exist in code. This is the engram interpreter: takes a SKILL_*_ENTRY memory id, loads its payload, walks the program cells, executes MCPCALL / FORKIF / ENDIF primitives, and returns a result. Habits pass the right engram id as basket context; cursor does the walking.

## Why this matters (biomimicry framing)

This is the exact direction Akien set 2026-04-13: 'disassemble what the LLM does in a way nobody has done before, move it one bit at a time into the graph matrix.' Right now /sprint and /commit run in the LLM (Claude) as skill markdown. The goal is Igor runs them natively — a PROC habit matches the trigger, dispatches to an engram interpreter, and the interpreter walks the program cells in the graph matrix rather than asking an LLM to do it.

The SKILL_*_ENTRY engrams are already there. The PROC_INVOKE_* habits are already there. The interpreter is the piece that finishes the circuit. Without it, the graph has the skill knowledge but can't act on it.

## Scope

1. **pe_chain.run_engram_cursor(basket)** — reads basket['skill_engram_id'] or resolves one from the habit's metadata. Loads the SKILL_*_ENTRY memory. Walks payload.run_cell program. Dispatches MCPCALL to mcp or registry.execute; FORKIF evaluates a condition and branches; ENDIF rejoins. Other primitives as needed (see skill_importer.py for full list).
2. **Error handling** — any primitive failure logs forensically and returns a structured error; never crashes the dispatch path.
3. **Habit dispatch wiring** — PROC_INVOKE_SPRINT and PROC_INVOKE_COMMIT need to pass the right SKILL_*_ENTRY id in the dispatch basket. Either: a) hardcode in the habit's metadata, b) infer from the habit name (PROC_INVOKE_SPRINT → SKILL_SPRINT_ENTRY), c) use a new dispatch_to_engram metadata field.
4. **Tests** — walk a mock SKILL_*_ENTRY with a trivial program, assert MCPCALL / FORKIF / ENDIF behave correctly, assert failures degrade gracefully.

## Existing work to check
- **tools/skill_importer.py** — _build_payload, MCPCALL + FORKIF + ENDIF primitive grammar
- **tools/pe_chain.py** — existing chain interpreter pattern (pe_entry_init, pe_claim, pe_read_ticket, etc.) — use the same basket-driven flow
- **tools/skill_filter.py** — related plan-verification engram, another candidate for the cursor interpreter
- **T-skill-to-engram-generalise** (2026-03-26b) — the parent epic this work sits under
- **D260 payload-as-program** — the schema
- **Memory.payload property** — cell named by triggers.values(); NARRATIVE=embedding source; data fields=constants this node brings; executor touches only cell fields

## Dependencies
- T-goals-as-persistent-relationships (#422, done) — goal facia are one path into sprint/commit dispatch once Igor can identify what he's working on
- T-concurrent-ne-spawn — future; engram cursor could run in a spawned COA so sprint/commit don't monopolize the main conversing COA

## Out of scope
- Rewriting /sprint or /commit as Igor-native behavior (that's the end-state, not this ticket)
- Adding new skills to the importer
- Touching CC's .claude/skills/ files

## Update 2026-04-14 evening — engrams must be CP-consistent

Akien 2026-04-14: engrams must be derivable from, or at minimum consistent with, the six core patterns (CP1-CP6). The interpreter in this ticket should NOT blindly execute any engram — it should verify (or the upstream loader should verify) that engrams honor:

- **CP1:** no engram asserts certainty in its outputs. Outputs are provisional.
- **CP2:** when the engram fails, the failure is captured as learning (not a crash).
- **CP3:** the engram has a causal structure — it explains why it does what it does.
- **CP6:** the engram doesn't assume safety of inputs. Untested inputs are flagged.

**Becomes a registered audit check** once T-audit-add-checks is mature: "no engrams in the SKILL_*_ENTRY set that violate the six CP patterns." The check validates by walking each engram's payload cells and confirming the CP-consistency properties are present.

**Operational consequence:** the interpreter rejects (or sandboxes) engrams that fail CP validation. Adding a new skill engram (via skill_importer) must pass CP validation before it's stored as a SKILL_*_ENTRY memory.
body.title: Engram cursor runtime — walk SKILL_*_ENTRY program cells in the matrix
body.result: Extracted cursor traversal from main.py → cursor_runtime.py. run_cursor() with CursorResult. Loop detection, max_steps safety, error handling, fork dispatch. MCPCALL already implemented. main.py reduced by 90 lines. 9 tests.
